creator: bdrewery@FreeBSD.org
text:
> On Apr 23, 2014, at 3:47, Tijl Coosemans <tijl@FreeBSD.org> wrote:
>=20
>> On Tue, 22 Apr 2014 10:47:35 -0500 Bryan Drewery wrote:
>> Results at=20
>> http://package18.nyi.freebsd.org/bulk/91amd64-default-pr_188759/2014-04-2=
1_01h39m12s
>>=20
>> New failures:
>>=20
>> + {"origin"=3D>"databases/firebird21-client", "phase"=3D>"build",=20
>> "errortype"=3D>"cluster"}
>> + {"origin"=3D>"games/nethack32", "phase"=3D>"build",=20
>> "errortype"=3D>"process_failed"}
>=20
> These two seem to be false positives.  The firebird port Makefile
> compares a list of semaphores in post-build with the list saved during
> pre-build.  Then it removes any new semaphore that appeared during
> do-build.  This can easily go wrong if poudriere is building another
> port that uses semaphores at the same time, such as another firebird
> port.

Ah nice find! We will need to fix that separately.

>=20
> The nethack failure looks like a problem in its makefiles.  It builds
> some files in src (monst.c, objects.c).  Then runs a submake on ../util
> which in turn runs a submake back on ../src for those same files (the
> target scripts for these files remove files under ../util).  I think
> gmake can get confused because of this and remake some files (and thus
> remove files under ../util again) when it shouldn't have.  Normally I
> would look into this more, but this is a game from the nineties and it's
> very stage unfriendly.
>=20
>> + {"origin"=3D>"deskutils/notify-osd", "phase"=3D>"build",=20
>> "errortype"=3D>"linker_error"}
>> + {"origin"=3D>"math/giacxcas", "phase"=3D>"build",=20
>> "errortype"=3D>"linker_error"}
>> + {"origin"=3D>"x11-fm/nautilus", "phase"=3D>"package",=20
>> "errortype"=3D>"cluster"}
>=20
> These were valid and I've uploaded a new patch at
> http://people.freebsd.org/~tijl/keepla.patch
>=20
> This patch includes many more PORTREVISION bumps because bapt has been
> adding a lot of USES=3Dlibtool in the past few days.
>=20
> Do you want to do another exp-run or can this be committed?

I don't think we need another. Go for it.=
